texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: VA-Oncology Lung Template User Guide
texts.text: VA-Oncology Lung Template User Guide

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Background Information
texts.text: Background Information

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The National Oncology Program Office seeks to provide tools to enable providers to format essential clinical data within text progress notes so that it is readily available for clinical care and to measure the quality of cancer care in VA in a prospective, continuous, timely, and cost- efficient manner. Using Reminder Dialog Templates in CPRS is one mechanism to achieve these goals.
texts.text: The National Oncology Program Office seeks to provide tools to enable providers to format essential clinical data within text progress notes so that it is readily available for clinical care and to measure the quality of cancer care in VA in a prospective, continuous, timely, and cost- efficient manner. Using Reminder Dialog Templates in CPRS is one mechanism to achieve these goals.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Oncology reminder dialog templates will include the necessary clinical information to measure standard quality indicators. Quality indicators are selected from those endorsed by the National Quality Forum, used in ASCO's Quality Oncology Practice Initiative, and other sources.
texts.text: Oncology reminder dialog templates will include the necessary clinical information to measure standard quality indicators. Quality indicators are selected from those endorsed by the National Quality Forum, used in ASCO's Quality Oncology Practice Initiative, and other sources.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1 General Information
texts.text: 1 General Information

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this guide is to provide the end-user with directions on how to perform medical documentation using the VA-Oncology Lung Template. The VA-Oncology Lung Template was developed and is supported by the National Oncology Program Office in Specialty Care Services (part of Patient Care Services). REDACTED.
texts.text: The purpose of this guide is to provide the end-user with directions on how to perform medical documentation using the VA-Oncology Lung Template. The VA-Oncology Lung Template was developed and is supported by the National Oncology Program Office in Specialty Care Services (part of Patient Care Services). REDACTED.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Recommended Users
texts.text: Recommended Users

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The primary users of the Lung Cancer Template are providers in services of medical oncology, radiation oncology, and surgical oncology. Secondary uses may include pulmonologists, oncology nurses, cancer care coordinators, palliative care specialists, and cancer registrars.
texts.text: The primary users of the Lung Cancer Template are providers in services of medical oncology, radiation oncology, and surgical oncology. Secondary uses may include pulmonologists, oncology nurses, cancer care coordinators, palliative care specialists, and cancer registrars.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2 Installation
texts.text: 2 Installation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The facility Clinical Applications Coordinator (CAC)/Health Information Specialist (HIS) at each site has received instructions on how to load and implement the VA-Oncology Lung Template. These instructions are part of the PXRM_2_0_38_IG which accompanies this patch. Its primary intended use is as a template that is pulled into an open progress note rather than as a standalone note. The VA-Oncology Lung Template should be available in a shared folder in the template drawer in CPRS. The location and naming convention for the template and shared folder will vary by site. Users should contact their local help desk or local CAC/HIS if the VA- Oncology Lung Template cannot be located in CPRS.
texts.text: The facility Clinical Applications Coordinator (CAC)/Health Information Specialist (HIS) at each site has received instructions on how to load and implement the VA-Oncology Lung Template. These instructions are part of the PXRM_2_0_38_IG which accompanies this patch. Its primary intended use is as a template that is pulled into an open progress note rather than as a standalone note. The VA-Oncology Lung Template should be available in a shared folder in the template drawer in CPRS. The location and naming convention for the template and shared folder will vary by site. Users should contact their local help desk or local CAC/HIS if the VA- Oncology Lung Template cannot be located in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Facilities might choose to link the template to a specific standalone note title rather than use it as a template that is inserted into an open progress note. However, doing so might limit the availability of the template to the range of providers who could make use of the template.
texts.text: Facilities might choose to link the template to a specific standalone note title rather than use it as a template that is inserted into an open progress note. However, doing so might limit the availability of the template to the range of providers who could make use of the template.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users will need to contact their local CAC/HIS determine the local name of the note.
texts.text: Users will need to contact their local CAC/HIS determine the local name of the note.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3 Usage
texts.text: 3 Usage

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is recommended that the VA-Oncology Lung Template be initiated and used for newly- diagnosed lung cancer cases and that information is updated periodically throughout the course of care (see documentation section

The template is not intended to be a chart abstraction tool that is only filled in at the end of care, but rather to be used concurrently with delivery of care throughout the patient’s course. When the template is opened on subsequent visits, previously documented information is viewable (see documentation section

However, once a section is begun, all subsections must either be completed or the check removed from the top level of that section.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4 Documentation:
texts.text: 4 Documentation:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The VA-Oncology Lung Template is separated into three sections: diagnosis, treatment and post- treatment.
texts.text: The VA-Oncology Lung Template is separated into three sections: diagnosis, treatment and post- treatment.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5 Definitions for Each Section Diagnosis Section
texts.text: 5 Definitions for Each Section Diagnosis Section

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Date of first abnormal radiology
texts.text: Date of first abnormal radiology

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Date of first abnormal radiology prior to the initial diagnosis of lung cancer that documented suspicion of lung cancer. For example, date of first abnormal radiology could be 3 years prior to diagnosis when observation with serial CTs began or when lesion enlarged enough to warrant further workup.
texts.text: Date of first abnormal radiology prior to the initial diagnosis of lung cancer that documented suspicion of lung cancer. For example, date of first abnormal radiology could be 3 years prior to diagnosis when observation with serial CTs began or when lesion enlarged enough to warrant further workup.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 6 Date of diagnosis
texts.text: 6 Date of diagnosis

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Date first procedure performed that confirmed the initial pathologic diagnosis of lung
texts.text: Date first procedure performed that confirmed the initial pathologic diagnosis of lung

texts.label: text
texts.orig: cancer (e.g. date of biopsy). If no biopsy was done, provide date that provider made decision to treat as lung cancer.
texts.text: cancer (e.g. date of biopsy). If no biopsy was done, provide date that provider made decision to treat as lung cancer.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 7 Clinical stage
texts.text: 7 Clinical stage

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Stage based on physical examination, imaging tests, and/or biopsies done in the absence of pathological staging procedures. For NSCLC, select a Summary Stage 0-IV, OR a TNM Stage. For SCLC, select Summary Stage then indicate limited or extensive, or TNM Stage. Include the date the stage was determined.
texts.text: Stage based on physical examination, imaging tests, and/or biopsies done in the absence of pathological staging procedures. For NSCLC, select a Summary Stage 0-IV, OR a TNM Stage. For SCLC, select Summary Stage then indicate limited or extensive, or TNM Stage. Include the date the stage was determined.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 8 Pathological stage
texts.text: 8 Pathological stage

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Staging done after lung resection surgery or invasive mediastinal staging. For NSCLC, select a Summary Stage 0-IV, OR a TNM Stage. For SCLC, select Summary Stage then indicate limited or extensive, or TNM Stage. Include the date the stage was determined.
texts.text: Staging done after lung resection surgery or invasive mediastinal staging. For NSCLC, select a Summary Stage 0-IV, OR a TNM Stage. For SCLC, select Summary Stage then indicate limited or extensive, or TNM Stage. Include the date the stage was determined.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 9 Histology
texts.text: 9 Histology

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Indicate histology of tumor.
texts.text: Indicate histology of tumor.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 10 Molecular Testing:
texts.text: 10 Molecular Testing:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: EGFR Mutational Analysis and ALK translocation test. Indicate if test was done and result.
texts.text: EGFR Mutational Analysis and ALK translocation test. Indicate if test was done and result.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 11 ECOG Performance Status
texts.text: 11 ECOG Performance Status

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ECOG Scale definitions:
texts.text: ECOG Scale definitions:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 0 = Fully active, able to carry on all pre-disease performance without restriction
texts.text: 0 = Fully active, able to carry on all pre-disease performance without restriction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 1 = Restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature, e.g., light house work, office work
texts.text: 1 = Restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature, e.g., light house work, office work

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2 = Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to carry out any work activities. Up and about more than 50% of waking hours
texts.text: 2 = Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to carry out any work activities. Up and about more than 50% of waking hours

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 3 = Capable of only limited self-care, confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking hours 4 = Completely disabled. Cannot carry on any self-care. Totally confined to bed or chair
texts.text: 3 = Capable of only limited self-care, confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking hours 4 = Completely disabled. Cannot carry on any self-care. Totally confined to bed or chair

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 5 = Dead
texts.text: 5 = Dead

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Treatment Section
texts.text: Treatment Section

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If tumor board available has case been presented?
texts.text: If tumor board available has case been presented?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If tumor board available, indicate whether or not case has been discussed. If discussed, include the date of the first tumor board discussion. If no tumor board available, select N/A.
texts.text: If tumor board available, indicate whether or not case has been discussed. If discussed, include the date of the first tumor board discussion. If no tumor board available, select N/A.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 12 Surgery performed? (Stages I, II, III)
texts.text: 12 Surgery performed? (Stages I, II, III)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If surgery was performed, indicate type and outcome of surgery, along with the date of surgery. If surgery was not performed, select reason.
texts.text: If surgery was performed, indicate type and outcome of surgery, along with the date of surgery. If surgery was not performed, select reason.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 13 Lymph node sampling (Stages I, II, III)
texts.text: 13 Lymph node sampling (Stages I, II, III)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If LN sampling done, indicate procedure and date. If not, specify reason.
texts.text: If LN sampling done, indicate procedure and date. If not, specify reason.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 14 Radiation therapy administered
texts.text: 14 Radiation therapy administered

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Indicate if radiation therapy was administered. If yes, indicate intent, if the intent was discussed with the patient, site of radiation, date therapy started, and if stereotactic body radiation was administered. If radiation therapy was not administered indicate reason. This section is used later to indicate when radiation has stopped and the reason it was stopped.
texts.text: Indicate if radiation therapy was administered. If yes, indicate intent, if the intent was discussed with the patient, site of radiation, date therapy started, and if stereotactic body radiation was administered. If radiation therapy was not administered indicate reason. This section is used later to indicate when radiation has stopped and the reason it was stopped.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 15 Chemotherapy administered
texts.text: 15 Chemotherapy administered

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Indicate if chemotherapy was administered. If yes, indicate intent, if the intent was discussed with the patient, name of chemotherapy drug(s) administered, and the dates each chemotherapy drug was begun. If platinum-based doublet chemo was not administered, provide the reason why not. If the patient has a non-squamous cell NSCLC histology and bevacizumab was not administered, indicate a reason why bevacizumab was not administered. If no chemotherapy was administered indicate reason why.
texts.text: Indicate if chemotherapy was administered. If yes, indicate intent, if the intent was discussed with the patient, name of chemotherapy drug(s) administered, and the dates each chemotherapy drug was begun. If platinum-based doublet chemo was not administered, provide the reason why not. If the patient has a non-squamous cell NSCLC histology and bevacizumab was not administered, indicate a reason why bevacizumab was not administered. If no chemotherapy was administered indicate reason why.

texts.text: Post-Treatment Section

texts.text: Result of most recent follow-up

texts.text: Indicate the result(s) of most recent follow-up including the patient’s status and the date of this assessment

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 16 Did the patient receive referral for hospice or palliative care (Stage III or IV)
texts.text: 16 Did the patient receive referral for hospice or palliative care (Stage III or IV)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Indicate if patient received a referral for hospice/palliative care, and the date of that referral.
texts.text: Indicate if patient received a referral for hospice/palliative care, and the date of that referral.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 17 Initial Entries:
texts.text: 17 Initial Entries:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users should document only in sections that are pertinent to their patient. There are no required fields at this point. However, once a section is opened, users are required to answer all questions within that section, or uncheck the box for that section (in which case no information from that section will be included).
texts.text: Users should document only in sections that are pertinent to their patient. There are no required fields at this point. However, once a section is opened, users are required to answer all questions within that section, or uncheck the box for that section (in which case no information from that section will be included).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: a. Missing Required Fields:
texts.text: a. Missing Required Fields:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Whenever a section is opened the user is required to answer all questions in that section. If the user clicks on finish without answering all questions in a section a pop-up box will appear directing the user back to the specific unanswered question. For example, the ECOG PS section is not a required entry unless the user clicks on the checkbox for that section. Once that section is opened the user is required to select a score from the list. If the user clicks on finish without selecting an ECOG score the user will get the following error message:
texts.text: Whenever a section is opened the user is required to answer all questions in that section. If the user clicks on finish without answering all questions in a section a pop-up box will appear directing the user back to the specific unanswered question. For example, the ECOG PS section is not a required entry unless the user clicks on the checkbox for that section. Once that section is opened the user is required to select a score from the list. If the user clicks on finish without selecting an ECOG score the user will get the following error message:

texts.orig: 19 Header
texts.text: 19 Header

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Previously entered information in the VA-Oncology Lung Template will display in a header. The Header information will be included in the progress note text and is similar to information that is often included manually at the top of oncology provider notes.
texts.text: Previously entered information in the VA-Oncology Lung Template will display in a header. The Header information will be included in the progress note text and is similar to information that is often included manually at the top of oncology provider notes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Header is broken into three sections mimicking the template: diagnosis, treatment, and post-treatment. Each Header item will display up to 10 entries in descending order.
texts.text: The Header is broken into three sections mimicking the template: diagnosis, treatment, and post-treatment. Each Header item will display up to 10 entries in descending order.
